title: Sour Gooey Brazilian Mulukhiyah Salad
description: A bright, tangy salad that balances the earthy flavor of mulukhiyah with a creamy cashewcoconut sauce and a splash of lime for a quick, rawfriendly meal.

ingredients.name: Fresh mulukhiyah leaves
ingredients.notes: If frozen, thaw and squeeze out excess water

instructions: Soak cashews in water for 30 minutes.
Blend soaked cashews with 2 tbsp coconut milk, 1 tbsp water, 1/2 tsp salt, and a squeeze of lime juice until silky smooth to make the cashew cream.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add a splash of olive oil. Sauté minced garlic and grated ginger for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the mulukhiyah leaves to the skillet and stir to coat with oil and aromatics. Cook, stirring frequently, until the leaves wilt but retain a slightly firm bite, about 2–3 minutes.
Stir in lime juice, tamarind paste, salt, black pepper, and red chili flakes. Cook for an additional 1 minute to blend flavors.
Remove the skillet from heat and let the mixture cool slightly (about 2 minutes).
Transfer the warm mulukhiyah to a bowl and fold in the prepared cashew cream until the leaves are glossy and coated, creating a gooey consistency.
Transfer the salad to a serving platter, drizzle any remaining lime juice over the top, and sprinkle chopped cilantro for freshness.
Garnish with toasted cashews or peanuts for crunch.
Serve immediately for the best texture and flavor.
